rulings.Case Title: ROBERT A RATTI ET AL VS. RONALD J RATTI SR ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Confirming Sale Of Real Property
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Jul 01, 2020 line 1. OTHER JOHN MCINERNEY III's Motion For Order Confirming Sale Of Real Property READ THIS NOTIFICATION IN ITS ENTIRETY. Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Motion for Order Confirming Sale of Real Property. Hearing Required. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: YU YUN TIEN VS. MICHAEL TIEN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement; Request For Attorneys Fees And Costs Against Defendants Michael Tien And Sumei Tien
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Jul 01, 2020 line 2. PLAINTIFF YU YUN TIEN's Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement; Request For Attorneys Fees And Costs Against Defendants Michael Tien And Sumei Tien READ THIS NOTIFICATION IN ITS ENTIRETY. Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Plaintiff's Motion to Enforce Settlement Agreement. Hearing Required. By no later than 8:30 a.m. on July 1, 2020 parties to submit responses to the following questions: (1) If hypothetically, three offers received for purchase of the property were for $1, $2, and $3 respectively, would the parties have to rank the offers and agree on one or could the parties have rejected all offers; and (2) If the matter goes to the referee, would the referee be (a) re-marketing the property; (b) would the referee be choosing from the offers generated by Plaintiff's agent; or (c) would the referee be considering offer(s) generated later by the Defendant's agent. Briefs should be limited to the questions listed above. All the responses shall reference the specific language of the agreement relied upon and supported by authority. Briefs shall be filed, served on opposing counsel and courtesy copy provided to the Court to Dept501CourtesyCopies@sftc.org. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: ALAN C MCCLAY ET AL VS. LULA M MCCLAY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Discharge Referee And Approved Partition Referees Final Report Of Sale Of Real Property By Private Sale
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Jul 01, 2020 line 4. OTHER JOHN MCINERNEY's Amended Motion To Discharge Referee And Approved Partition Referees Final Report Of Sale Of Real Property By Private Sale READ THIS NOTIFICATION IN ITS ENTIRETY. Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. CCP 1005, CCP 1010.6, Emergency Rule 12. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: BARBARA VELARDE ET AL VS. MOSAICA 601 HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION ET AL

If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. rulings.Case Title: CATHERINE HERRERA WATSON VS. TONY GARNICKI
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Jul 1, 2020 line 6. DEFENDANT CHARLES MA's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION READ THIS NOTIFICATION IN ITS ENTIRETY. rulings.Case Title: SOHUI WON VS. JOHN DOE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Jul 1, 2020 line 7. DEFENDANT DANTE GERVACIO, CARMEN VILLAR, CELSO MACARIO, MILAGROS PACHECO, EDUARDO CIUDAD, MORENA GONZALEZ, DINORA LOPEZ, ABEL MEJIA, ELBA HERNANDEZ, MIGUEL ARGUETA and BRYAN MARROQUIN's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT READ THIS NOTIFICATION IN ITS ENTIRETY. Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant's Motion for Summary Judgment. Hearing Required. By no later than 8:30 p.m. Plaintiff to file a supplemental declaration addressing the requirements of CCP 437c(h) ("facts essential to justify opposition may exist but cannot, for reasons stated, be presented"), i.e. what facts Plaintiff believes exists, which discovery responses may provide those facts, and which issues raised in the motion will be disputed by those facts. Plaintiff to provide specific reference (a) the issue raised; (b) discovery to be obtained from a particular defendant; and (c) responses currently in Plaintiff's possession. E.g. (a) whether Defendant A is a tenant; (b) special interrogatory number XX, quote the interrogatory; and (c) response to special interrogatory number XX. Supplemental declaration \shall be filed, served on opposing counsel and courtesy copy provided to the Court to Dept501CourtesyCopies@sftc.org. = (501/CFH)

Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Jul 1, 2020 line 11. DEFENDANT ROBERT OF-THE-KINANN-FAMILY's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT READ THIS NOTIFICATION IN ITS ENTIRETY. Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant's Demurrer to Complaint is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Defendant failed to establish that the Federal Fair Debt Collection Practices Act applies to this case, and further, that Plaintiff was required serve a "debt validation notice" along with the 3-day notice.= (501/CFH)
